title: Mixer Initialization, Channels, Latency, Volume, Mute, and Cue Alternatives · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved game trace contains one plausible state, timing, input, vector, rendering, collision, ownership, audio, accessibility, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded accessible audio and cue system and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure mixer frequency, format, channels, buffer, and initialization before loading sounds and record platform limits.
Reserve or prioritize channels for critical cues and define overlap, stealing, cooldown, and device-loss behavior.
Provide master and separate effects and music control, mute, remembered settings, and safe defaults.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Mixer Init
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Asteroids team handles case 1926 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to own mixer initialization, channel budgets, latency, volume and mute controls, and non-audio alternatives for every critical cue; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Configure mixer frequency, format, channels, buffer, and initialization before loading sounds and record platform limits.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Audio Failure
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-audio-failure-sfarpa" that uses case 7212 to own mixer initialization, channel budgets, latency, volume and mute controls, and non-audio alternatives for every critical cue. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Audio Failure.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Audio Failure into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Audio Failure, keep Audio Failure connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-audio-failure-sfarpa
# Competency: asteroids-audio-failure.
# Case 7212: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_audio_failure_sfarpa(critical_events: int = 5, visual_alternatives: int = 5, channels_reserved: int = 3, clipped_sounds: int = 0, mute_available: bool = True, separate_volume: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_sfarpa = "sfarpa-aprafs-7212"
    assert evidence_variant_sfarpa
    evidence_axes_sfarpa = (
        "playersfarpa",
        "seed7212",
        "inputaprafs",
        "framesfarpa7212",
        "state7212sfarpa",
        "playtestaprafs7212",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_sfarpa)) == 6
    if critical_events < 0 or channels_reserved < 1:
        return False, critical_events, "audio-domain"
    if visual_alternatives != critical_events:
        return False, visual_alternatives, "cue-alternative"
    if clipped_sounds > 0:
        return False, clipped_sounds, "channel-starvation"
    if not mute_available or not separate_volume:
        return False, channels_reserved, "audio-control"
    assert visual_alternatives == critical_events
    return True, channels_reserved, "accessible-audio"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
